title: Escobaria asperispina
taxonomy: Escobaria asperispina is a species of cactus, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Caryophyllales, and the family Cactaceae. Within this family, it is placed in the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. Further classification places it in the subtribe Cactinae and the genus Escobaria. The species classification is as a single species, Escobaria asperispina. It is also sometimes referred to as Pediocactus asperispinus, but this is an older synonym.

intro: Escobaria asperispina, also known as the spiny pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Escobaria asperispina include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
